# CiteVahti

> CiteVahti tests every claim in a manuscript against its candidate papers through a blinded human → AI → adjudication workflow, then writes verified references to Zotero in a decision-gated, undoable step with a hash-chained audit trail. It is free, local-first, and single-user for researchers; Vahtian sells paid infrastructure to organizations that need auditable citation integrity at publication scale. A product of Vahtian.

## What it is

- **Citation-integrity tool**, not a reference manager. The manuscript is the code; each scientific claim is a test case checked against the paper cited for it.
- **Blinded second rating.** The human rates support first; the AI rates independently and stays hidden until the human has rated. Disagreements route to the human.
- **The human is always the decider.** AI values are advisory, never decisive, never silently propagated.
- **Decision-gated, undoable Zotero write.** Preview → confirm → write → undo, with fail-closed dedupe — never a silent or fabricated citation.
- **Hash-chained audit trail**, so the workflow can be reported transparently in a methods section.

## How it runs

- Local-first, single-user, no telemetry. Manuscript text and ratings stay on the user's machine.
- `citevahti start` launches the workspace: an inline review side panel, a browser, and an MCP server.
- Two co-primary surfaces: a chat client (Claude Desktop / ChatGPT / Claude Code / Codex) over MCP, and a loopback side panel that is the blind human-rating surface.
- Outbound calls are limited to literature services — PubMed (NCBI), OpenAlex, Semantic Scholar, Crossref/doi.org — and the user's Zotero if connected.

## Licensing

- Open core is Apache-2.0 and never paywalls a researcher's ability to verify their own manuscript.

## Links

- Site: https://vahtian.com/citevahti
- Source (GitHub): https://github.com/heidihelena/citevahti
- README / how it works: https://github.com/heidihelena/citevahti#readme
